evals.prompt: Make an ASCII banner for 'CHARM' with a double border in cyan.
evals.expected_output: Runs charmascii CLI with --border double --color cyan (font can be left default or reasonable), shows resulting terminal ASCII art to user.
assertions: Command text argument is exactly CHARM, unmodified
Response shows the rendered ASCII art to the user, not just the command

evals.prompt: make my project name 'nova' look cool in the terminal, something bold
evals.expected_output: Interprets vague style request into concrete flags (e.g. --font doom/block, maybe a border), runs charmascii, shows result. Should not just dump raw flag docs at the user.
assertions: Chooses a bold-style font (doom, block, banner, bulbhead, or 3-d) rather than leaving default standard font
Uses terminal output (no --output png/svg/txt/json) since request was for terminal display
Command text argument is exactly nova, unmodified
Response briefly explains or shows reasoning for the style choice, not just raw output

evals.prompt: I want a big bold logo saying 'ACME' for my landing page, save it as an image
evals.expected_output: Runs charmascii with --font doom/big, --output png (or svg), sensible --bg-color/--color contrast, saves file and tells user the path.
assertions: Chooses a big/bold font (doom, big, block, banner, bulbhead, or 3-d)
--bg-color and --color are set to contrasting values (not identical, not both dark or both light)
An actual image file exists on disk after the command runs
Response tells the user the file path where the image was saved
